key_insight: DrugGen-2 is a disease-aware generative model that uses disease ontology plus target protein sequences to design new small molecules for drug discovery.
clinical_relevance: By incorporating disease context, the approach may better prioritize candidate compounds for targets involved in complex diseases and support more informed lead generation.
method: Fine-tuned a pretrained GPT-2 model on approved drugs linked to diseases and targets.
Used supervised fine-tuning followed by GRPO reinforcement learning.
Optimized for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and predicted binding affinity.
evidence_signal: In evaluations on five targets relevant to diabetic nephropathy, DrugGen-2 outperformed DrugGPT and DrugGen, generated more unique molecules, and achieved stronger predicted docking affinities than reference drugs in several cases.
watch_item: Results rely on predicted affinity and docking rather than experimental validation, so real-world activity, safety, and developability remain unconfirmed.
paper_title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
